steps.title: Understand the bracket structure
steps.content: In single-elimination play, each match removes exactly one player. With 240 starters paired into 120 matches, 120 players are eliminated in round 1, leaving 120 to advance. Each subsequent round halves the field again.

steps.title: Count the eliminations per round
steps.content:
Track the eliminated count $p$ for each round $r$:
  • Round 1: $240/2 = 120$ eliminated.
  • Round 2: $120/2 = 60$ eliminated.
  • Round 3: $60/2 = 30$ eliminated.
  • Round 4: $30/2 = 15$ eliminated.

steps.title: Find the formula
steps.content: Each round-$r$ value equals $240 \cdot \left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^r$. Verify: $r=1$ gives 120, $r=2$ gives 60, $r=3$ gives 30, $r=4$ gives 15 — all match. So $$p = 240\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^r.$$

steps.title: Evaluate the choices
steps.content:
• A. $p=15\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^r$ — at $r=1$ gives 7.5, wrong.
  • B. $p=15(2)^r$ — at $r=1$ gives 30, wrong.
  • C. $p=240\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^r$ — matches every round.
  • D. $p=240(2)^r$ — grows instead of shrinking.
That matches choice C — C is correct.
